titleInfo.title: Zhenrong to Ruixiang: The Medieval Chinese Reception of the Mahābodhi Buddha Statue

mods.abstract: Choi Sun-ah calls the original at Mahābodhi Temple “true visage” zhenrong 真容, and the derivatives in China auspicious images ruixiang 瑞像. Buddha’s Shadow is also a true visage image. Puti ruixiang 菩提瑞像: originally main image of Mahābodhi Temple, Bodhgayā; Guangyuan 710-712, with Eulogy of the puti ruixiang; Sokkul-am, Kyongju, 8. century, Borobudur, Central Java, Indonesia, ca. 800; Banner of Famous Images (ruixiang) from Dunhuang Cave 17, New Delhi, 7th or 8th century? King Udāyana images 優填王像 (the first image) Sitting Longmen type at Wanfodong; Seiryōji (Kyoto) standing type; Gandhāran type (3rd century) with King Udāyana presenting his image. King Aśoka images: Excavated from Wanfo Si: standing figures with common features like protruding eyes and mustache, one dated ca. 551
